archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Top-level CI pipeline orchestration script — entry point for the CI process
Integration module for Claude API — service layer for external AI API interaction
Shared help/usage text utility at orchestration level
Adaptive analysis logic for the agent — business logic for adjusting agent behavior
Review analysis step in agent pipeline — business logic for reviewing agent output
Spin/loop detection analysis — agent behavior analysis logic
Stuck detection analysis — agent state analysis logic
Summary generation analysis step — agent output summarization logic
Workflow analysis for the agent — orchestrates analysis pipeline steps
Agent completion handling — determines when/how agent tasks complete
Heartbeat/keepalive utility for long-running agent lifecycle management
Task display/rendering utility for agent lifecycle output
Token budget management logic — tracks and enforces LLM token limits
Token usage tracking and reporting utility
Prompt construction for agent planning — assembles LLM prompts from briefs
Agent tool definitions and dispatch — registers and routes tool calls
CLI help text module for hench commands
Command guard/validation logic — enforces allowed commands policy
Guard contracts — runtime validation shapes defining guard policies
Path guard logic — validates and restricts file path access
Policy enforcement module — central guard policy evaluation logic
Metrics collection for concurrent process execution
Shell execution utility — wraps shell command spawning
Process execution utility — core exec abstraction for spawning processes
Process lifecycle management — handles start/stop/monitor of child processes
Process concurrency limiter — shared infrastructure for controlling parallel execution
Monitors memory usage — ongoing business logic that observes and reports system state
Throttling logic based on memory pressure — shared utility controlling execution rate
Process pool management — orchestrates lifecycle of worker processes
Tracks per-process memory — stateful service maintaining process metrics
Manages task execution queue — stateful orchestration of queued work items
Formatting helpers for queue output — pure presentation utility
Priority-based task scheduling — business logic for ordering execution
Shared glob pattern helpers — reusable file-matching utility
Dispatches tool calls — central routing logic for agent tool invocations
Shell execution wrapper — low-level utility for running shell commands
File operation tool implementations — utility wrappers around filesystem ops
Git operation helpers — utility wrappers around git commands
Shell tool abstraction — shared utility for shell interaction
Test runner tool — orchestrates test execution as an agent tool
Validates task completion criteria — business logic for determining done-ness
API-based LLM provider implementation — service that calls LLM REST APIs
Authentication helpers — shared utility for API key resolution and auth
CLI-based LLM provider — service that drives LLM via subprocess CLI
Codex CLI provider — service adapter for OpenAI Codex CLI
Factory that instantiates the correct LLM client — service entry point for client creation
Process execution utility — low-level helper for spawning subprocesses
Help text formatting — pure presentation utility
JSON parsing/streaming helpers — shared utility for LLM response handling
Core LLM client implementation — central service orchestrating LLM interactions
LLM configuration loading and resolution — config module for vendor/model settings
LLM type definitions and interfaces — pure type companion to the client
Project-level config reader — reads .n-dx.json and related project settings
Pure constants mapping project directory names — no logic, only exported values.
Defines LLMProvider interface, supporting types, and enums — no implementation.
ProviderRegistry class manages provider factory registration and instantiation — business logic orchestration.
ProviderSession class tracks active provider state and handles vendor switching — stateful service layer.
Pure string-processing utilities: edit distance, typo suggestions, formatting — no side effects.
Parsing/normalization utilities converting multiple token usage formats to a canonical shape.
File I/O for acknowledged findings store — hash computation, load/save, and state mutation.
Shared constants, prompt strings, JSON extraction helpers, and token accumulators used across the analyze module.
Post-LLM guard that triggers a secondary consolidation call when task count exceeds a ceiling — business logic orchestration.
Recursive LLM-driven task decomposition — orchestrates multi-pass traversal with Zod-validated responses.
Pure deduplication algorithms: bigram similarity, union-find clustering, action-verb normalization — no I/O.
Pure formatting utility that generates a human-readable diff tree comparing proposals to an existing PRD.
Document parsing pipeline: structured extraction from markdown/text with optional LLM disambiguation.
Pure file validation: extension checks, binary magic-byte detection, format-specific syntax warnings.
Interactive spec builder with LLM clarification rounds and readline-based user interaction — workflow orchestration.
Module-level LLM client singleton and configuration — manages provider wiring and prompt dispatch.
LLM-powered proposal modification via natural-language feedback — business logic with prompt building and quality validation.
Builds hierarchical proposal structures from flat scan results using multi-strategy task-to-feature matching.
Core LLM reasoning pipeline for PRD generation — Zod schemas, proposal parsing, quality validation, and re-exports for backward compat.
Fuzzy matching against existing PRD to filter already-tracked proposals and detect update candidates.
LLM-powered PRD restructuring pipeline — merge/split/reparent/obsolete proposals with multi-mode prompt strategies.
Multi-source scanning: tests, docs, sourcevision output, package.json — data fetching and aggregation with anti-pattern detection.
Pure validation and heuristic classification of modification requests — no I/O or side effects.
CLI help text definitions and formatting helpers — presentation data with no command execution logic.
Implements MCP tool handler functions — converts tool arguments to core function calls and formats responses, analogous to HTTP route handlers.
MCP server factory — registers all tools and resources, creates server instance for both HTTP and stdio transports.
Pure input validation and parsing utilities for CLI arguments — level validation, format checks, safe integer parsing.
Pure conflict detection and similarity scoring algorithms — no I/O, stateless scoring functions.
Atomically creates PRD items from recommendations with conflict handling, reparenting, and store writes.
Single exported constant — a markdown string defining the default agent workflow — no logic.
Defines built-in archetype constants and weighted signal patterns — pure type/constant definitions with no logic
Business logic service that classifies PRD items by change significance using heuristics
Service that collects completed PRD items by diffing git branches and reading PRD state
Pure filtering utility for work items based on branch lifecycle timestamps
Persistent JSON storage layer for branch-scoped work records with Zod validation — high fan-in store pattern

Builds call graphs via TypeScript AST parsing — core analysis service
File classification engine that scores files against archetype signals — core business logic service
LLM API client bridge using @n-dx/llm-client — external API service wrapper
Reads and extracts completion metadata from PRD state — data access service
